word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ɪn ɐ mˈɪliən θˈaʊzənd bˈɪliən jˈɪɹz

senses.definition: used to say that the chances of something happening or being true are very slim

examples.x: Not in a thousand years did I expect him to actually show up on time.
That cat is never in a million years going to accept the new puppy.

senses.definition: the slightest chance of something happening or being true

examples.x: With the current economic conditions, the small startup had a snowball's chance in hell of surviving.
She had a snowball's chance in hell of winning the race, as she was competing against much more experienced runners.
When he applied for the job without the required qualifications, he knew he had a snowball's chance in hell of getting hired.
The underdog team had a snowball's chance in hell of defeating the reigning champions, but they gave it their all.
Attempting to negotiate with that unreasonable landlord was like expecting a snowball's chance in hell of success.

senses.definition: something that seems impossible to occur

examples.x: Asking for a salary increase of 500% was like requesting a trip to the sun; it wasn't going to happen.
Trying to finish a month's worth of work in a single day seemed as challenging as planning a trip to the sun.
Her dream of becoming a famous singer overnight was compared to desiring a trip to the sun, both unattainable.
Hoping to win the lottery without buying a ticket is as unlikely as embarking on a trip to the sun.
Suggesting a picnic in the middle of a thunderstorm was considered the equivalent of planning a trip to the sun in terms of impracticality.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that cannot be done or obtained in any way

examples.x: And you still can't get a good daily woman now to clean, not for love or money.
I can’t get hold of that book for love nor money.

senses.definition: something that is very good or desirable but highly unlikely to happen or be real

examples.x: This talk of moving to Australia is all just pie in the sky.
The politician's pledge to solve all the nation's problems overnight was dismissed as mere pie in the sky.
is dreams of becoming a rock star and touring the world appeared to be just pie in the sky until he got a record deal.
Promising an effortless path to success is often likened to offering pie in the sky, as it rarely delivers real results.

word_list.pronunciation: baɪ ˌɛni nˈoʊ stɹˈɛtʃ ʌvðɪ ɪmˌædʒᵻnˈeɪʃən

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is impossible or is extremely hard to imagine or obtain

examples.x: By no stretch of the imagination does the factory operate efficiently.
This could not by any stretch be seen as a victory.
By no stretch of the imagination can we consider this project a success; it was a complete failure.
By any stretch of the imagination, it's impossible to see how this proposal benefits the environment.
Her artistic abilities are exceptional, and by any stretch of the imagination, she's a talented painter.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something is impossible, unacceptable, or absolutely will not happen under any conditions

examples.x: No way are you borrowing my laptop after last time!
I'd no way trust someone who lies so easily.
You're no way getting my keys after you totaled the car last week.
No way can we afford a vacation this year.
They'll no way finish the project by Friday without help.

word_list.pronunciation: ðɪ ɔːɹ ðæt ʃˈɪp hɐz lˈɑːŋ ɔːɹ sˈeɪld

senses.definition: used to say that an offer or possibility is not renewable or available any more

examples.x: If you were hoping to ask Mollie out, that ship has long sailed—she's going to the dance with Peter.
He wanted to invest in that company years ago, but the ship has long sailed, and the stock price has skyrocketed.
They had considered buying the beachfront property, but they delayed the decision, and now it's too expensive; the ship has sailed.
He thought about confessing his feelings, but now she's in a committed relationship with someone else; the ship has sailed on that romance.

senses.definition: to be unable to do or finish something without being harmed or suffering its consequences

examples.x: We're already over budget on this project, and we can ill afford any additional expenses.
She's working two part-time jobs to make ends meet, so she can ill afford any unexpected bills right now.
He had a tight schedule and could ill afford any distractions if he wanted to meet his deadlines.

senses.definition: the time when it becomes impossible for one to return to a previous place or state or to make a different decision

examples.x: After signing the contract, they had reached the point of no return, and there was no way to cancel the deal.
The team's performance in the first half of the game was so poor that they had already crossed the point of no return.
When they boarded the plane, they knew it was the point of no return; there was no going back.
She realized that divorcing her spouse was the point of no return in their relationship, and there was no reconciliation.
Accepting the job offer meant relocating to a new city, marking the point of no return in her career.

senses.definition: not allowed or possible

examples.x: Going on vacation this summer is out of the question due to work commitments.
When asked about a promotion, he realized it was out of the question since he lacked the necessary qualifications.
Borrowing his friend's antique car for the road trip was out of the question; it was far too valuable.
The proposal to extend the project's deadline was out of the question, as it was already behind schedule.
Considering his severe allergies, having a pet with fur was out of the question for him.

senses.definition: said to mean that it is impossible for something to ever happen

examples.x: "I'll lend you my vintage car when chickens have teeth," he said, doubting he would ever let anyone borrow it.

senses.definition: used to express disbelief in something happening

examples.x: "I'll lend you my vintage sports car when hell freezes over," he replied when asked for a loan.
She promised to give up her favorite dessert, but that will happen when hell freezes over.

senses.definition: used for saying that it is completely unlikely that something ever happens

examples.x: "He'll admit he was wrong when it's a cold day in hell," his friends said, acknowledging his stubbornness.
The chances of her quitting her favorite job are as likely as a cold day in hell; she loves it too much.
Getting him to apologize is about as probable as a cold day in hell; he rarely admits fault.
Convincing the strict professor to cancel the exam is impossible; it's like waiting for a cold day in hell.
The likelihood of her adopting a cat, despite being allergic to them, is as remote as a cold day in hell.

senses.definition: used to say that one thinks that something will never happen

word_list.pronunciation: ˌoʊvɚ wˈʌnz dˈɛd bˈɑːdi ɔːɹ kˈɔːɹps

senses.definition: used to show that one will do whatever one can to stop something from happening

examples.x: Over his dead body would he let her be blindsided by a man who caused her so much pain and worry.
Over my dead body will you drive home after you've been drinking!
Over my dead corpse!
She has told people in private that over her dead body will that happen.
She moves into our home over my dead body.

senses.definition: to desire what cannot be achieved

examples.x: Asking for a raise right after starting a new job is like crying for the moon; you need to prove your worth first.
His wish for a private jet for his birthday is nothing more than crying for the moon; it's far beyond their budget.
Wanting a perfect relationship without any disagreements is like crying for the moon; conflicts are a part of every relationship.
The customer's demand for a custom-made item within an hour is crying for the moon; it's impossible to produce in such a short time.
Hoping for a complete overhaul of the healthcare system without any financial consequences is like crying for the moon; it's a complex issue with no easy solution.

senses.definition: (of a proposal, topic, or offer) unavailable or incapable of being considered

examples.x: After hours of negotiation, the idea of a pay raise was off the table for the foreseeable future.
Discussing the controversial policy was off the table, as it was clear that it wouldn't gain the necessary support.

senses.definition: a hope or wish that cannot be realized or fulfilled

examples.x: His dream of becoming a billionaire without putting in any effort is nothing but a castle in the air.
Building a successful career without any education or training is akin to constructing a castle in the sky.
She spent hours imagining a world where all her problems disappeared, but she knew it was just a castle in the air.
The idea of winning the lottery and retiring on a private island is a castle in the sky for most people.
Hoping to become a famous singer without singing a note is like building a castle in the air; it requires hard work and dedication.

senses.definition: used to emphasize that something is completely impossible or prohibited

examples.x: The car wouldn't start this morning, so it's a no-go for our road trip.
Trying to repair the old computer was a no-go; it was beyond repair.
The plan to have a surprise party for her birthday is a no-go now that she knows about it.
